title: Enforce a data contract between producer and consumer
description: Codify the schema, semantics, and SLAs a producer guarantees so a breaking change is rejected at the source, not discovered downstream.
body: Use when a dataset is shared across teams or pipelines. Do not let producers change shape or semantics unilaterally — consumers break without warning and blame lands on the wrong team. Actions: define a contract (schema, types, nullability, value semantics, freshness SLA), validate producer output against it in CI/at publish, and version it; reject or quarantine violations. Validation signals include a contract check passing at publish and a breaking change blocked before reaching consumers. Risk boundary: an unversioned, unenforced contract makes every producer change a potential outage; require contract validation on shared datasets and consumer sign-off for breaking changes.
